# Project Context: hebbian-decay

<!-- Auto-generated by /cortex-spec from spec artifacts. -->

## Tech Stack

- Python 3.12 — all scripts
- SQLite (stdlib `sqlite3`) with WAL mode — `graph.db`, `atoms.db`
- No new external dependencies for this slug

## Conventions

- LLM calls: `env -u ANTHROPIC_API_KEY claude -p` (never bare `claude -p`)
- Embeddings: local only (`all-MiniLM-L6-v2`); never sent to API
- Vault root: `~/memory/vault/`; `graph.db` is a symlink to active snapshot
- Atomic writes: WAL mode; never bypass with `PRAGMA journal_mode=DELETE`
- Decay discriminator: `WHERE description='hebbian'` — never touch other edges

## Architecture Rules

- Decay runs AFTER increment in the same `vault chunk` call: increment → decay
- `apply_decay()` receives `fired_entity_ids` from `update_from_atoms()` — never re-query atoms.db inside `apply_decay()`
- Prune (DELETE) edges below MIN_WEIGHT=0.01 — do not leave floating-point noise
- No schema changes to `relations` table

## Write Boundaries

- `~/memory/vault/scripts/hebbian.py` — primary implementation
- `~/memory/vault/bin/vault` — `cmd_chunk()` integration + `cmd_graph()` stats
- `graph.db` (via symlink) — UPDATE weight, DELETE pruned rows only
